(born March 1, 1952) is an American [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction"), [fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy "Fantasy"), and [mystery](/wiki/Mystery_fiction "Mystery fiction") writer. He has written novels, short fiction, screenplays for [television](/wiki/Television "Television"), scripts for comic books, animation, newspaper copy, and magazine articles.

**Steven Barnes** (born March 1, 1952) is an American [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction"), [fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy "Fantasy"), and [mystery](/wiki/Mystery_fiction "Mystery fiction") writer. He has written novels, short fiction, screenplays for [television](/wiki/Television "Television"), scripts for comic books, animation, newspaper copy, and magazine articles.

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Steven_Barnes&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Barnes, was born on March 1, 1952, in [Los Angeles, California](/wiki/Los_Angeles,_California "Los Angeles, California"). He has had a varied education, including a secondary education at [Los Angeles High School](/wiki/Los_Angeles_High_School "Los Angeles High School"). He continued at [Pepperdine University](/wiki/Pepperdine_University "Pepperdine University"), majoring in communication arts.[[1]](#cite_note-Black_&_White-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Steven_Barnes&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Barnes wrote several episodes of [*The Outer Limits*](/wiki/The_Outer_Limits_(1995_TV_series) "The Outer Limits (1995 TV series)") and *[Baywatch](/wiki/Baywatch "Baywatch")*. His "[A Stitch In Time](/wiki/A_Stitch_in_Time_(The_Outer_Limits) "A Stitch in Time (The Outer Limits)")" episode of *The Outer Limits* won an [Emmy Award](/wiki/Emmy_Award "Emmy Award"). He also wrote the episode ["Brief Candle"](/wiki/Stargate_SG-1_(season_1) "Stargate SG-1 (season 1)") for *[Stargate SG-1](/wiki/Stargate_SG-1 "Stargate SG-1")* and the *[Andromeda](/wiki/Andromeda_(TV_series) "Andromeda (TV series)")* episode ["The Sum of Its Parts"](/wiki/List_of_Andromeda_episodes#Season_1 "List of Andromeda episodes"). Barnes's first published piece of fiction, the 1979 [novelette](/wiki/Novella "Novella") "The Locusts", was written with [Larry Niven](/wiki/Larry_Niven "Larry Niven"), and was a [Hugo Award](/wiki/Hugo_Award "Hugo Award") nominee.[[2]](#cite_note-Barnes'_listing_in_''Locus''_magazine's_database_of_award_nominees-2)[[3]](#cite_note-Excerpts_of_a_March_2003_interview_with_Barnes_in_''Locus''_magazine-3) Barnes subsequently collaborated with Niven on several other books, including three books with both Niven and [Jerry Pournelle](/wiki/Jerry_Pournelle "Jerry Pournelle"). Barnes said he clashed politically with the two conservative writers but enjoyed working with them, calling it "a tremendous learning opportunity".[[4]](#cite_note-sh-interview-4)

Barnes's alternate history novel *[Lion's Blood](/wiki/Lion%27s_Blood "Lion's Blood")* won the 2003 [Endeavour Award](/wiki/Endeavour_Award "Endeavour Award").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] His 2004 [Star Wars](/wiki/Star_Wars "Star Wars") tie-in novel *[The Cestus Deception](/wiki/The_Cestus_Deception "The Cestus Deception")* was a [New York Times](/wiki/New_York_Times "New York Times") bestseller.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Together with his wife, [Tananarive Due](/wiki/Tananarive_Due "Tananarive Due"), and actor [Blair Underwood](/wiki/Blair_Underwood "Blair Underwood"), Barnes won the 2009 NAACP Image Award for outstanding Literary Work - Fiction for *In the Night of the Heat: A Tennyson Hardwick Novel*.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Steven_Barnes&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Barnes is married to [Tananarive Due](/wiki/Tananarive_Due "Tananarive Due"), a writer.[[4]](#cite_note-sh-interview-4) The couple live in [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles") and host a video blog together. They also taught a course together at UCLA called "The Sunken Place: Racism, Survival & Black Horror" that experienced a surprise visit from *[Get Out](/wiki/Get_Out "Get Out")* director [Jordan Peele](/wiki/Jordan_Peele "Jordan Peele"). The course is now available as a webinar.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Barnes has a daughter from his first marriage and a son from his current marriage.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Barnes is also an avid practitioner of [martial and physical arts](/wiki/Martial_arts "Martial arts"). He began studying in 1969.[[1]](#cite_note-Black_&_White-1) He is a [black belt](/wiki/Black_belt_(martial_arts) "Black belt (martial arts)") in [Kenpo Karate](/wiki/Kenpo "Kenpo") (BKF style), and [Kodokan Judo](/wiki/Judo "Judo"). He holds an instructor certificate in Wu Ming Ta, and has an instructor candidate ranking in Filipino [Kali](/wiki/Eskrima "Eskrima") stick and knife fighting. He is an advanced student in [Jun Fan](/wiki/Jeet_Kune_Do#Overview_and_philosophy "Jeet Kune Do") [kickboxing](/wiki/Kickboxing "Kickboxing") ([Bruce Lee](/wiki/Bruce_Lee "Bruce Lee") method under [Dan Inosanto](/wiki/Dan_Inosanto "Dan Inosanto")), and is an instructor in [Wu-style tai chi](/wiki/Wu-style_tai_chi "Wu-style tai chi") under Hawkins Cheung.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

He is an intermediate student in self-defense pistol shooting (preferring the [Turnipseed modified Weaver method](/wiki/Weaver_stance "Weaver stance")). He holds a [brown belt](/wiki/Obi_(martial_arts) "Obi (martial arts)") in [Shorenji Jiu Jitsu](/wiki/The_Jitsu_Foundation "The Jitsu Foundation"), and intermediate rankings in [Tae Kwon Do](/wiki/Tae_Kwon_Do "Tae Kwon Do") and [Aikido](/wiki/Aikido "Aikido"). He completed the Yoga Works basic [Hatha Yoga](/wiki/Hatha_Yoga "Hatha Yoga") instructor program; and is studying [Pentjak Silat](/wiki/Pentjak_Silat "Pentjak Silat") (an Indonesian fighting system) with Guru Stevan Plinck, and [Ashtanga Yoga](/wiki/Ashtanga_Vinyasa_Yoga "Ashtanga Vinyasa Yoga"), an aerobic form of yoga.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Bibliography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Steven_Barnes&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Bibliography")]

* The [Dream Park](/wiki/Dream_Park "Dream Park") series:
  + *Dream Park* (1981; with [Larry Niven](/wiki/Larry_Niven "Larry Niven"))
  + *The Barsoom Project* (1989; with [Larry Niven](/wiki/Larry_Niven "Larry Niven"))
  + *The California Voodoo Game* (1992; with [Larry Niven](/wiki/Larry_Niven "Larry Niven"))
  + *The Moon Maze Game* (2011; with [Larry Niven](/wiki/Larry_Niven "Larry Niven"))
* The Aubry Knight series:
  + *Street Lethal* (1983)
  + *Gorgon Child* (1989)
  + *Firedance* (1993)
* The Heorot series:
  + *[The Legacy of Heorot](/wiki/The_Legacy_of_Heorot "The Legacy of Heorot")* (1987; with [Larry Niven](/wiki/Larry_Niven "Larry Niven") and [Jerry Pournelle](/wiki/Jerry_Pournelle "Jerry Pournelle"))
  + *[The Dragons of Heorot](/w/index.php?title=The_Dragons_of_Heorot&action=edit&redlink=1 "The Dragons of Heorot (page does not exist)")* (1995; with [Larry Niven](/wiki/Larry_Niven "Larry Niven") and [Jerry Pournelle](/wiki/Jerry_Pournelle "Jerry Pournelle"))
  + *[Beowulf's Children](/wiki/Beowulf%27s_Children "Beowulf's Children")* (1995; with [Larry Niven](/wiki/Larry_Niven "Larry Niven") and [Jerry Pournelle](/wiki/Jerry_Pournelle "Jerry Pournelle"))
  + *Starborn and Godsons* (2020; with [Larry Niven](/wiki/Larry_Niven "Larry Niven") and [Jerry Pournelle](/wiki/Jerry_Pournelle "Jerry Pournelle"))
* The Insh'Allah series:
  + *[Lion's Blood](/wiki/Lion%27s_Blood "Lion's Blood")* (2002; winner of the 2003 [Endeavour Award](/wiki/Endeavour_Award "Endeavour Award"))
  + *[Zulu Heart](/wiki/Zulu_Heart "Zulu Heart")* (2003)
* The "Ibandi" series:
  + *Great Sky Woman* (2006)
  + *Shadow Valley* (2009)
* The Tennyson Hardwick Novels:
  + *[Casanegra](/wiki/Casanegra_(novel) "Casanegra (novel)")* (2007; with [Blair Underwood](/wiki/Blair_Underwood "Blair Underwood") and [Tananarive Due](/wiki/Tananarive_Due "Tananarive Due"))
  + *In the Night of the Heat* (2008; with Blair Underwood and Tananarive Due)
  + *From Cape Town with Love* (2010; with Blair Underwood and Tananarive Due)
  + *South by Southeast* (September 2012; with Blair Underwood and Tananarive Due)
* Stand-alone novels, screenplays, and other works:
  + *[The Descent of Anansi](/wiki/The_Descent_of_Anansi "The Descent of Anansi")* (1982; with Larry Niven)
  + "[To See the Invisible Man](/wiki/To_See_the_Invisible_Man "To See the Invisible Man")" (1986; a television script adapting a short story by [Robert Silverberg](/wiki/Robert_Silverberg "Robert Silverberg"), for the 1980s revival of *[The Twilight Zone](/wiki/The_Twilight_Zone_(1985_TV_series) "The Twilight Zone (1985 TV series)")*)
  + *The Kundalini Equation* (1986)
  + *[Fusion](/wiki/Fusion_(Eclipse_Comics) "Fusion (Eclipse Comics)")* (1987) (issues #1–5 only)
  + *Achilles' Choice* (1991) (with Larry Niven)
  + *Blood Brothers* (1996)
  + *Iron Shadows* (1997)
  + *[Far Beyond the Stars](/wiki/Far_Beyond_the_Stars "Far Beyond the Stars")* (1998) (*[Star Trek: Deep Space Nine](/wiki/Star_Trek:_Deep_Space_Nine "Star Trek: Deep Space Nine")* novelization)
  + *[The Lives of Dax](/wiki/The_Lives_of_Dax "The Lives of Dax")*: "The Music Between the Notes" (1999)
  + *Saturn's Race* (2000) (with [Larry Niven](/wiki/Larry_Niven "Larry Niven"))
  + *Charisma* (2002)
  + *[The Cestus Deception](/wiki/The_Cestus_Deception "The Cestus Deception")* (2004) (*[Star Wars](/wiki/Star_Wars "Star Wars")* novel set in the [Clone Wars](/wiki/Clone_Wars_(Star_Wars) "Clone Wars (Star Wars)"))
  + *Assassin and Other Stories* (2010), a collection, [ISFiC Press](/wiki/ISFiC_Press "ISFiC Press")
  + *The Invisible Imam*, a novel included in *Assassin and Other Stories* (2010)
  + *Devil's Wake* (2012; with Tananarive Due)
  + *Domino Falls* (2013; with Tananarive Due)
  + *Star Wars Saved My Life: Be the HERO in the adventure of your lifetime!* (2015), illustrated by Trey Jackson and Angelina Greenwood
  + *The Seascape Tattoo* (2016; with [Larry Niven](/wiki/Larry_Niven "Larry Niven"))
  + *Twelve Days* (2017)
  + *Star Wars: Mace Windu: The Glass Abyss* (2024) (*[Star Wars](/wiki/Star_Wars "Star Wars")* novel set shortly after [The Phantom Menace](/wiki/The_Phantom_Menace "The Phantom Menace"))
* Short stories
  + "The Woman in the Wall" (2000; novelette)
  + "Danikil", *Whose Future Is It?* (2018)[[7]](#cite_note-7)
